channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: SQL Joins & Window Functions: The Skills That Separate Analysts from Beginners
item.description: You have learned the basics of SQL - 'SELECT', 'WHERE', 'GROUP BY' and now you are ready to level up: to move from I know SQL to I can analyze data. I will help you understand Joins and Window...

item.title: CytoScnPy - Fast Python Static Analysis
item.description: CytoScnPy is a high-performance static analysis tool for Python built to help developers understand, clean, and secure their codebases with speed and precision. At its core, CytoScnPy combines the...

item.title: Separating API and Application Services in Ruby with Servactory
item.description: Every Rails codebase I've worked on eventually needs to talk to an external API. Stripe for payments, Slack for notifications, some internal microservice for data sync. And every time, the same...

item.title: TPU Logic for Architects: When to Choose Accelerated Compute Over Traditional CPUs
item.description: The Rack2Cloud AI Infrastructure Series The software world treats AI like just another API call. But beneath the abstraction, AI is the heaviest, most latency-sensitive, and hardware-dependent...

item.title: A Ascensão dos Agentes: Dominando o Contexto e a "Sopa de Letrinhas" da IA no Desenvolvimento com Github Copilot
item.description: Se você trabalha com desenvolvimento de software, já percebeu que a Inteligência Artificial deixou de ser um mero "papagaio de código" — aquele autocompletar glorificado — para se tornar um...

item.title: RFC 8693 Deep Dive: Token Exchange
item.description: Introduction In modern system architectures, particularly in microservices architectures, the following scenarios are incredibly common: Conversion at the API Gateway: You want to exchange a...

item.title: CLAUDE.md Best Practices: 7 formatting rules for the Machine
item.description: I watched an agent ignore a rule I wrote 2 hours earlier. Not a vague rule. A specific one. "run pytest before committing." It was right there in the CLAUDE.md, paragraph two, between the project...

item.title: Why Product Tours Should Live in Your Git Repo
item.description: Why Product Tours Should Live in Your Git Repo Most product tour tools have the same problem: they charge per user, lock your content in their dashboard, and have no answer for what happens when...

item.title: 5 Web Dev Pitfalls That Are Silently Killing Your Projects (With Real Fixes)
item.description: Most of us have shipped something that "worked on my machine" only to watch it fall apart in production. The frustrating part? Beginner projects tend to fail in the same areas: mobile UX,...

item.title: Building a Rails Engine #7 — The Orchestrator: Coordinating the Workflow
item.description: The Orchestrator: Coordinating the Import Workflow How to coordinate parsing, validation, and persistence through a single class that manages state transitions, handles errors per-record, and...

item.title: LLMs can unmask pseudonymous users at scale with surprising accuracy
item.description: Pseudonymity has never been perfect for preserving privacy. Soon it may be pointless.

item.title: On Moltbook
item.description: The MIT Technology Review has a good article on Moltbook, the supposed AI-only social network: Many people have pointed out that a lot of the viral comments were in fact posted by people posing as...

item.title: AI Agents: The Next Wave Identity Dark Matter - Powerful, Invisible, and Unmanaged
item.description: The Rise of MCPs in the Enterprise The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming a practical way to push LLMs from “chat” into real work. By providing structured access to applications,...

item.title: Starkiller Phishing Suite Uses AitM Reverse Proxy to Bypass Multi-Factor Authentication
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new phishing suite called Starkiller that proxies legitimate login pages to bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA) protections. It's...

item.title: This startup claims it can stop lightning and prevent catastrophic wildfires
item.description: On June 1, 2023, as a sweltering heat wave baked Quebec, thousands of lightning strikes flashed across the province, setting off more than 120 wildfires. The blazes ripped through parched forests...

item.title: Big Google Home update lets Gemini describe live camera feeds
item.description: Google Home chief Anish Kattukaran announced several updates to the smart home platform that fix a long list of annoyances and idiosyncrasies. There's also one noteworthy new addition: the...

item.title: Microsoft Warns OAuth Redirect Abuse Delivers Malware to Government Targets
item.description: Microsoft on Monday warned of phishing campaigns that employ phishing emails and OAuth URL redirection mechanisms to bypass conventional phishing defenses implemented in email and browsers. The...

item.title: Google Confirms CVE-2026-21385 in Qualcomm Android Component Exploited
item.description: Google on Monday disclosed that a high-severity security flaw impacting an open-source Qualcomm component used in Android devices has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in question is...

item.title: SloppyLemming Targets Pakistan and Bangladesh Governments Using Dual Malware Chains
item.description: The threat activity cluster known as SloppyLemming has been attributed to a fresh set of attacks targeting government entities and critical infrastructure operators in Pakistan and Bangladesh. The...

item.title: Cursor has reportedly surpassed $2B in annualized revenue
item.description: The four-year-old startup saw its revenue run rate double over the past three months, according to one Bloomberg source.

item.title: What Is That Mysterious Metallic Device US Chief Design Officer Joe Gebbia Is Using?
item.description: Gebbia was reportedly spotted at a San Francisco coffee shop using an unidentified pair of earbuds with a circular disc that looks similar to the device seen in a recent OpenAI hoax ad.

item.title: This AI Agent Is Ready to Serve, Mid-Phone Call
item.description: Deutsche Telekom, the German cell provider—which holds a majority stake in T-Mobile—is partnering with ElevenLabs to enable an AI assistant on all of its network’s calls in Germany. No app required.

item.title: ChatGPT uninstalls surged by 295% after DoD deal
item.description: Many consumers ditched ChatGPT's app after news of its DoD deal went live, while Claude's downloads grew.

item.title: No one has a good plan for how AI companies should work with the government
item.description: As OpenAI transitions from a wildly successful consumer startup into a piece of national security infrastructure, the company seems unequipped to manage its new responsibilities.

item.title: Anthropic upgrades Claude’s memory to attract AI switchers
item.description: Anthropic is making it easier to switch to its Claude AI from other chatbots with an update that brings Claude's memory feature to users on the free plan, along with a new prompt and dedicated...

item.title: Apple’s cheapest AirPods have hit $89
item.description: It’s a good day for Apple fans on a budget. Not only did the company introduce a new budget phone, its entry-level AirPods 4 are also on sale. Right now, you can snag a pair of Apple’s AirPods 4...

item.title: How the new iPhone 17E stacks up against Apple’s pricier phones
item.description: The iPhone 17 lineup now has a “budget” option. Announced this week, the iPhone 17E will hit stores on Wednesday, March 11th, starting at $599, with preorders opening on March 4th. The 6.1-inch...

item.title: Apple might use Google servers to store data for its upgraded AI Siri
item.description: Apple has asked Google to look into "setting up servers" for a new version of Siri that's Gemini-powered and meets Apple's privacy requirements, The Information reports. Apple had already...

item.title: Mastodon now has a button for sharing content from other websites
item.description: Sharing content to Mastodon is getting easier with the addition of a universal "Share to Mastodon" button for the decentralized social media platform. Announced on Monday, the widget allows users...

item.title: You could be an influencer without even realizing it
item.description: In late February, Puck reported on a strange case: An influencer with more than a million followers was inadvertently promoting products on Instagram. On some of Julia Berolzheimer's posts, a...
